proposals.title: Adaptive mutation operator portfolio
proposals.rationale: Use committed lineage outcomes to adapt operator choice and mutation magnitude, converting accumulated evidence into sustained gains while preserving deterministic, bounded execution.
acceptance: Every candidate and accepted receipt records an operator ID and version, deterministic seed, and bounded mutation parameters.
Identical frontier, policy, fitness epoch, and receipt history produce identical operator choices and candidate bytes.
Operator credit changes only after committed outcomes and preserves a policy-configured nonzero exploration rate.
Repeated improvements from one operator increase its selection frequency without exceeding autonomy budgets.

proposals.title: Stagnation detection and recovery
proposals.rationale: As the frontier rises, fixed local search can plateau indefinitely; explicit progress windows and deterministic recovery modes keep evolution advancing without masking failure.
acceptance: Progress is measured over committed transitions using floor gains for classic selection and niche gains for quality-diversity selection.
Crossing a policy-defined stagnation threshold emits exactly one content-addressed plateau event.
Recovery deterministically selects an allowed action such as operator rotation, mutation-radius expansion, or underexplored-lineage targeting.
A committed improvement exits recovery mode, and replay reproduces every plateau and recovery decision.

proposals.title: Multi-parent crossover with DAG lineage
proposals.rationale: Recombining complementary genomes can escape mutation-only local optima, while explicit multi-parent ancestry preserves trustworthy provenance as lineages become DAGs.
acceptance: A crossover child references at least two eligible parent content digests plus an operator version, seed, and crossover mask.
Replay reconstructs byte-identical child genomes from the recorded parents and crossover metadata.
Validation rejects missing parents, duplicate parents, ancestry cycles, and parents ineligible under the active selection policy.
Crossover children must satisfy the same validation, fitness, and replacement margins as mutation children.

proposals.title: Cross-epoch fitness calibration
proposals.rationale: Versioned scoring protects historical truth but leaves long-term progress across epochs incomparable; immutable calibration artifacts can bridge epochs without rewriting receipts.
acceptance: Each supported epoch scores a content-addressed sentinel corpus into a calibration artifact keyed by corpus and evaluator digests.
Repeated calibration with identical inputs produces byte-identical output.
Historical receipt scores remain unchanged while cross-epoch views use calibrated paired deltas or explicitly report incomparability.
Activating a new epoch fails closed unless configured rank-correlation and regression thresholds pass.

proposals.title: Lineage diversity guardrails
proposals.rationale: Phenotype niches can still be occupied by one dominant dynasty; ancestry concentration controls preserve independent evolutionary options and reduce frontier-wide correlated failure.
acceptance: The lineage builder deterministically reports founder distribution, effective lineage count, and ancestry concentration for the active frontier.
Selection distinguishes phenotypically different descendants of one founder from candidates with independent ancestry.
Policy-configured concentration limits reject lineage-collapsing replacements unless a documented fitness override margin is met.
Every lineage-diversity decision records its metrics, threshold, and override result in the committed receipt.

proposals.title: Deterministic council consensus convergence
proposals.rationale: Repeated insufficient-consensus and no-consensus cycle artifacts justify deterministic proposal reconciliation and one bounded runoff without forcing a winner or weakening the exact-eight requirement.
acceptance: Versioned deterministic rules canonicalize equivalent fixture proposals while receipts preserve every original feature_id.
A first pass without quorum triggers at most one policy-budgeted runoff by the same eight strategies over an immutable shortlist.
Fewer than eight valid runoff responses, an unresolved tie, or an unmet threshold produces an explicit no-consensus result.
Identical snapshots and responses produce byte-identical decision receipts.

proposals.title: Crash-safe resumable orchestration
proposals.rationale: Provider calls, validation, and commits span multiple stages; durable checkpoints prevent crashes from duplicating calls, consuming budgets twice, or producing divergent outcomes.
acceptance: Forced termination after each orchestration stage can resume to the same final artifacts as an uninterrupted run.
Resume rejects mismatched repository revisions, policy digests, or provider configurations.
A hash-verified completed provider response is reused without issuing another provider call.
The final mutation commits exactly once across repeated resume attempts.

proposals.title: Cross-process orchestration run leases
proposals.rationale: Scheduled workflows and local brainstem commands can overlap; a fenced lease protects the whole run before provider calls, complementing artifact-level atomic transactions.
acceptance: A second process targeting the same warehouse revision fails closed before making a provider call.
A stale lease is reclaimable only after its bounded timeout and receives a higher fencing token.
A former lease holder cannot commit after losing its lease.
Independent warehouse roots can execute concurrently.

proposals.title: Policy-controlled provider circuit breaker and failover
proposals.rationale: Bounded retries limit one invocation but do not prevent repeated rounds from hammering an unavailable brainstem; persisted health state and explicit failover keep autonomy bounded and observable.
acceptance: The configured failure threshold opens a persisted circuit and suppresses further calls during its cooldown.
Fallback from the local brainstem to Copilot CLI or offline generation occurs only when explicitly allowlisted by policy.
Every skipped, probe, and fallback attempt records provider, reason, consumed budget, and circuit state in the receipt.
A half-open circuit permits one probe, and failure of all allowed providers returns an explicit error without mutation.

proposals.title: Council-to-Copilot work-order handoff
proposals.rationale: A revision-bound, policy-gated work order can connect accepted council decisions to GitHub Copilot CLI execution while preserving operator control and preventing automatic publication or merge.
acceptance: An accepted proposal exports a schema-validated work order containing its source receipt, pinned revision, files, acceptance criteria, and policy digest.
Default operation only writes the local work order and performs no subprocess, network, commit, push, or merge action.
Dispatch is denied before invoking Copilot CLI unless policy explicitly grants it and sufficient runtime and call budgets remain.
Enabled dispatch runs against an isolated worktree at the pinned revision and records exit status plus diff digest without automatically merging or pushing.

proposals.title: Least-privilege, pinned GitHub Actions
proposals.rationale: Scheduled and issue-triggered workflows bridge untrusted input to repository write authority; explicit permissions, immutable dependencies, and separated validation/write jobs reduce token and supply-chain exposure.
acceptance: Every workflow declares read-only default permissions, with write permissions granted only to the job that requires them.
Every external action reference is pinned to a full commit SHA.
Issue-controlled values are never interpolated directly into shell commands or workflow expressions that execute code.
An automated test rejects workflows with implicit write permissions, unpinned actions, or write-capable jobs reachable before validation.

proposals.title: Quarantine and pin external submissions
proposals.rationale: Public issue and gist ingestion crosses an internet-to-warehouse trust boundary; transport restrictions and immutable source pinning must precede parsing or persistent mutation.
acceptance: Ingestion accepts only approved HTTPS gist endpoints and revalidates every redirect while rejecting private, loopback, and link-local destinations.
Fetches enforce deterministic timeout, response-size, and content-type limits before parsing.
Accepted submissions record the immutable gist revision, canonical source URL, and SHA-256 digest of fetched bytes.
Malformed, oversized, redirected, or digest-mismatched submissions leave cards, frontier, lineage, and evolution files byte-for-byte unchanged.

proposals.title: Role-scoped lineage operation authorization
proposals.rationale: A valid signature proves key possession, not authority; lineage mutations need action-, resource-, and role-scoped authorization with revocation and replay protection.
acceptance: Lineage creation binds an owner identity, and update, promote, resolve, or delete operations require an authorized owner or explicitly configured maintainer role.
Signed intents bind the operation type, target lineage, current revision, expiry, and unique nonce; replay and cross-operation reuse are rejected.
Unknown, expired, revoked, or wrong-scope keys fail closed without mutating repository state.
Key rotation preserves verification of historical receipts while immediately preventing revoked keys from authorizing new operations.

proposals.title: Verified peer catalog federation
proposals.rationale: Federated peer catalogs are outside the repository trust boundary and must not silently inject, replace, or impersonate local cards without pinned provenance.
acceptance: Each peer entry pins an HTTPS origin and either a catalog digest or trusted signing key.
Catalogs with invalid signatures, digest mismatches, unsupported schemas, expired metadata, or unsafe redirects are excluded entirely.
Remote identities cannot overwrite local card identifiers or another peer's namespace.
Every displayed remote card exposes its peer origin, catalog revision, and verification status, with deterministic tests covering forged and replayed catalogs.

proposals.title: Pin and sandbox the hologram runtime
proposals.rationale: Floating third-party iframe URLs make rendering behavior and card provenance mutable after publication; an immutable runtime reference and restrictive embedding boundary make artifacts reproducible and safer.
acceptance: Every generated animation URL references an immutable approved hologram runtime revision rather than a floating deployment root.
Cards and the build manifest record the runtime revision and integrity digest used to render them.
All hologram iframes use a restrictive sandbox, no-referrer policy, and only the minimum required capabilities.
Artifact generation fails when a runtime origin is unapproved, unpinned, or inconsistent with the recorded manifest.

proposals.title: Tactical Creature Battles
proposals.rationale: Convert the evolving population into a playable system where roster composition and turn choices matter beyond scalar strength rankings.
acceptance: A player can select three active-frontier creatures and battle a seeded AI squad.
Each turn offers legal actions derived from motion, articulation, and radiance traits with documented counter rules.
Identical frontier revision, squads, seed, and action sequence produce the same winner and turn log in Python and the browser.
Every completed battle exports a replay token that can reconstruct the match without changing frontier fitness.

proposals.title: Branching Evolution Campaign
proposals.rationale: Add run-based progression and consequential choices around the existing evolution mechanics, creating longer-term goals and varied replay paths.
acceptance: A campaign contains branching battle, recovery, and evolution nodes with at least two route choices per stage.
Players carry a finite squad and resources across encounters until victory or squad defeat.
Evolution nodes use the existing validated draft flow and require the player to choose among eligible outcomes.
The same campaign seed and decisions reproduce the same map, encounters, rewards, and ending.

proposals.title: Daily Seeded Challenges
proposals.rationale: Give players a recurring shared objective with constrained roster-building and deterministic scoring that rewards repeated optimization.
acceptance: The UTC date and pinned frontier revision deterministically select one challenge, opponent, and rule modifier.
Every challenge defines roster constraints, a scoring formula, and a maximum attempt duration before play begins.
The browser records the player's best verified score and replay for each date.
Past challenges remain replayable but cannot replace the current day's ranked result.

proposals.title: Seasonal Verified Leaderboards
proposals.rationale: Turn deterministic challenge performance into asynchronous competition while preserving trustworthy, inspectable outcomes.
acceptance: A leaderboard submission includes the season, challenge ID, frontier revision, score, and complete replay log.
The ingest workflow replays submissions and rejects any score, outcome, or digest mismatch.
Verified entries are ranked deterministically with documented tie-breaking rules and one best entry per player per challenge.
Closed seasons are immutable and remain viewable separately from the active season.

proposals.title: Player Progression and Cosmetic Unlocks
proposals.rationale: Provide persistent mastery goals across battles, campaigns, and collection play without introducing pay-to-win or competitive stat advantages.
acceptance: A versioned local profile tracks unique discoveries, battle milestones, campaign clears, and unlocked cosmetics.
Milestones unlock arena or card presentation options only and never modify competitive calculations.
Processing the same achievement event more than once is idempotent.
Profiles can be exported and imported with validation that rejects malformed or unsupported data.

proposals.title: Accessible web experience
proposals.rationale: The animation-heavy gallery, arena, lab, and observatory need consistent keyboard, screen-reader, focus, contrast, and reduced-motion behavior to remain usable beyond pointer-driven desktop browsing.
acceptance: Automated accessibility scans report zero critical or serious violations on every public HTML page.
Every interactive control is keyboard reachable and operable with a persistent visible focus indicator.
Pages expose named landmarks, labeled controls, logical heading order, and live announcements for asynchronous status changes.
With prefers-reduced-motion enabled, continuous decorative animation and automatic hologram activation stop without hiding content or controls.

proposals.title: Responsive mobile controls and layouts
proposals.rationale: Dense rankings, 3D interaction, evolution controls, and receipt views require deliberate touch and small-screen layouts rather than desktop pages merely shrinking.
acceptance: At viewport widths from 320 to 768 CSS pixels, each page has no unintended horizontal scrolling or clipped primary controls.
All primary touch targets are at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels and work without hover.
Arena and evolution controls remain reachable and do not obscure selected-organism details in portrait or landscape orientation.
Zooming to 200 percent preserves readable content and complete task flows without overlapping controls.

proposals.title: Viewport-budgeted hologram rendering
proposals.rationale: Rendering a live 100-frame iframe for every card can multiply network, memory, and animation costs as the population grows; viewport-aware activation would preserve the live-art experience without loading the whole catalog concurrently.
acceptance: With an 85-card fixture, no more than six live hologram iframes are attached concurrently during initial load or scrolling.
Offscreen cards retain fixed-size placeholders and activate their holograms when approaching the viewport.
Holograms far outside the viewport are suspended or detached and reactivate without losing card identity.
A mobile performance run records cumulative layout shift at or below 0.1 while browsing the full catalog.

proposals.title: Resilient loading, empty, and failure states
proposals.rationale: Static JSON, generated artifacts, and remote hologram embeds can fail independently; explicit progress, retry, empty, and degraded states prevent blank or misleading interfaces.
acceptance: Each asynchronous data view presents a labeled loading state before content resolves.
Missing, malformed, or empty warehouse and card data produces an actionable inline state instead of a blank view or uncaught exception.
Failed hologram embeds expose a stable fallback with a retry control while preserving card metadata.
Retrying after a simulated transient failure renders the requested content without reloading the page.

proposals.title: Privacy-preserving frontend diagnostics
proposals.rationale: The existing evolution observatory records product and lineage decisions, but operators still need visibility into browser-side data failures, embed failures, interaction latency, and rendering health across static pages.
acceptance: Every public page records page readiness, data-load duration, data-load failure, and hologram failure using one versioned event schema.
A debug mode displays and exports a bounded local diagnostic report containing page, component, revision, duration, and error category.
Diagnostic records exclude genome payloads, hologram tokens, URL query values, and persistent user identifiers.
The local event buffer is capped at 200 records and enabling diagnostics sends no network requests unless an explicit endpoint is configured.

proposals.title: Commit-reveal holdout fitness evaluation
proposals.rationale: The public deterministic objective remains optimizable even when robust. Commit to an evaluator-only perturbation seed before proposal generation, reveal it afterward, and require candidates to clear unseen robustness panels so metric-specific overfitting is costly while every decision remains replayable.
acceptance: Each challenge records a SHA-256 commitment to an evaluator seed before invoking the proposal worker.
The seed and selected holdout panel are absent from every proposal-worker request.
The candidate digest is fixed before the seed is revealed.
A receipt reveals the seed and independently reproduces every holdout score while verifying the commitment.
A fixture engineered to maximize only the canonical aggregate fails the holdout gate while a balanced control passes.

proposals.title: Adversarial fitness benchmark and epoch gate
proposals.rationale: Versioned fitness permits change but does not by itself prove that a new epoch resists known exploits. A frozen corpus of valid controls, pathological genomes, and metamorphic variants can block evaluator changes that reward stuffing, clipping, irrelevant metadata, or diversity collapse.
acceptance: Every selectable fitness epoch has a manifest naming its parent, benchmark digest, and measurable promotion thresholds.
CI fails when an epoch rewards any registered adversarial fixture above its paired balanced control.
Metamorphic tests prove that identity metadata and keyframe-preserving serialization changes cannot alter fitness.
The audit emits a deterministic machine-readable report covering objective floors, ranking stability, and diversity impact.
Attempting to activate an epoch without a passing benchmark report fails closed.

proposals.title: Pareto-aware multi-objective selection
proposals.rationale: A single blended strength allows extreme gains in one term to conceal meaningful regressions elsewhere. Retaining the objective vector and applying epsilon-Pareto replacement makes tradeoffs explicit and prevents compensatory metric gaming while preserving deterministic ranking.
acceptance: Fitness evaluation returns a named normalized objective vector alongside the aggregate score.
Replacement in an occupied niche requires epsilon-Pareto dominance and passes explicit vitality and quality floors.
The scalar aggregate is used only as a deterministic tie-break among otherwise eligible nondominated candidates.
Receipts record every objective delta, applicable epsilon, and the exact dominance decision.
A high-glow fixture with degraded vitality cannot replace a balanced parent despite having a higher legacy aggregate.

proposals.title: Continuous novelty search archive
proposals.rationale: Discrete biome-by-behavior niches preserve coarse diversity but can still converge within each cell. A continuous descriptor archive with nearest-neighbor novelty exposes unexplored behavior, resists near-clone churn, and explains why a candidate is genuinely different without weakening quality gates.
acceptance: Novelty is computed deterministically from descriptor distance to the same k nearest archived observations.
Archive admission uses a declared novelty threshold without bypassing existing quality requirements.
The archive is content-addressed and deterministically pruned to a configured maximum size.
Reordering identical observations produces the same archive digest and novelty values.
The observatory displays each candidate's novelty score, nearest neighbors, and admission reason.

proposals.title: Replayable fitness scorecards
proposals.rationale: Fitness formulas are documented, but operators need a decision-level explanation of gates, transformations, rewards, penalties, and thresholds. Structured scorecards make optimization pressure inspectable and let reviewers distinguish genuine improvement from one-term exploitation.
acceptance: Every evaluation emits a versioned scorecard containing raw metrics, normalized terms, weights, gates, penalties, final score, threshold, and outcome.
An independent verifier reconstructs the recorded final score from the scorecard within the declared numeric tolerance.
Rejected candidates identify the binding gate or largest negative contribution using deterministic rules.
Accepted candidates show parent-to-child deltas for every objective rather than only the aggregate change.
Python and browser scorecard renderings pass parity fixtures for every supported fitness epoch.

proposals.title: Concurrency-Safe Scheduled Mutations
proposals.rationale: Atomic local writes do not prevent harness and ingest workflow runs from racing or publishing work based on stale repository revisions.
acceptance: Both mutation workflows use one repository-wide concurrency group with cancellation disabled.
Every mutation verifies its expected repository and frontier revisions immediately before persistence.
A test running two writers from the same revision proves exactly one commits and the other reports a stale-revision conflict.
Concurrent-run tests leave the ledger, frontier, catalog, and build manifest mutually consistent.

proposals.title: Crash-Recovery Fault-Injection Suite
proposals.rationale: The transaction paths need executable evidence that interruption at every persistence boundary remains recoverable rather than corrupting durable state.
acceptance: Tests inject failures before and after each durable-write boundary used by evolution and ingestion.
After every injected failure, state is either the complete previous version or the complete new version and passes ledger verification.
The next invocation deterministically handles orphaned transaction files without duplicating an observation or receipt.
CI runs the fault-injection suite on every change to transaction or storage code.

proposals.title: Reproducible Artifact CI Gate
proposals.rationale: Numerous derived JSON and static artifacts are tracked, so unnoticed generator drift can publish a repository whose views disagree with canonical warehouse state.
acceptance: Building artifacts twice from a clean checkout produces byte-identical outputs and manifest digests.
CI regenerates all derived artifacts and fails with the stale file paths when the worktree differs.
Build results remain identical across two hash seeds and UTC versus a non-UTC timezone.
Manifest verification rejects altered inputs, outputs, generator versions, or schema versions.

proposals.title: Reversible Schema Migration Framework
proposals.rationale: Versioned fitness, frontier, lineage, and receipt formats require migration tooling that can be rehearsed and rolled back without rewriting immutable history.
acceptance: Every migration supports validation-only dry runs and reports exact records that would change.
Fixture tests cover migration from each supported historical schema to the current schema.
A failed migration leaves all tracked state byte-identical to its pre-migration form.
Rollback restores materialized state while preserving immutable observations and historical receipt digests.

proposals.title: Operational Telemetry and Workflow Watchdog
proposals.rationale: Scheduled autonomy needs structured run outcomes and explicit detection of missed or repeatedly failing workflows, not only domain receipts after successful work.
acceptance: Loop, ingest, and council commands emit one schema-versioned report containing revision, run identity, duration, outcome, mutation counts, budget use, and categorized failure.
Workflows upload the report and publish a GitHub step summary even when the command fails.
The watchdog detects missed schedules and consecutive failures against configured thresholds and creates only one deduplicated incident.
A successful recovery run resolves the incident, and telemetry tests verify that tokens and proposal payloads are never emitted.

proposals.title: Unified Product Navigation
proposals.rationale: The gallery, arena, lab, observatory, Holodex, and NFT views currently read as separate destinations. A shared information architecture would make them one coherent static product without expanding the evolution engine.
acceptance: All six product pages render the same navigation labels and ordering from one shared configuration.
Each page indicates its active destination and exposes one clear next action.
Existing page URLs and embedded hologram URLs remain valid.
Navigation is keyboard-operable and has no horizontal overflow at a 320px viewport.

proposals.title: Guided First Evolution
proposals.rationale: Interactive evolution already exists, but first-time visitors must understand unfamiliar vocabulary and disconnected surfaces before seeing its value. A seeded, read-only walkthrough can deliver an evidence-backed first success without creating another evolution implementation.
acceptance: A fresh visitor can start the walkthrough from the homepage and reach a completed example lineage in five or fewer explicit steps.
The walkthrough defines Moment, frontier, fitness, double-jump, and receipt using one committed lineage.
The walkthrough performs no repository writes, publishing, authentication, or provider calls.
Users can skip, restart, and continue from the walkthrough into the real Arena, Evolution Lab, and Observatory.

proposals.title: Federated Evolution Challenges
proposals.rationale: Holodex federation shares cards, but the repository's strongest differentiation is verified evolution. A static, opt-in challenge exchange would let trusted peers contribute candidates while keeping the local deterministic referee authoritative and avoiding a central service.
acceptance: A canonical challenge document includes the target digest, frontier revision, fitness epoch, leap margin, challenge ID, and expiry.
Imported peer candidates pass through the existing validation, scoring, policy, and atomic transaction path without federation-specific shortcuts.
Stale revisions, unsupported schemas, invalid provenance, and untrusted peers are rejected without changing warehouse state.
Accepted receipts record the originating peer, challenge ID, and candidate digest.
Federation remains explicitly invoked and unavailable peers never block local evolution.

proposals.title: Explainable Fitness Deltas
proposals.rationale: The scoring system is rigorous, but scalar strengths obscure why a candidate won. Consistent component explanations would turn existing robustness into understandable product differentiation and help users trust each jump.
acceptance: Python exposes named component contributions, gates, bonuses, and penalties for every supported fitness epoch.
Reported contributions reconcile with the final score within a documented numeric tolerance.
Evolution views show parent-to-child component deltas, the required leap margin, and the exact acceptance reason.
Python and browser explanations match for shared fixtures without changing canonical historical scores.

proposals.title: Installable Double Jump CLI
proposals.rationale: The current quickstart requires cloning the repository and knowing Python module paths. A packaged command, diagnostics, and bounded demo would reduce operator friction while preserving the repository-based architecture and existing scope.
acceptance: Installing a built wheel in a clean virtual environment exposes a double-jump command.
double-jump doctor validates workspace schemas, policy, optional providers, and write permissions with actionable failures and nonzero exit codes.
double-jump demo creates an isolated sample workspace and completes one seeded offline jump without modifying the source checkout or publishing.
Existing python3 -m harness.loop commands remain supported and produce equivalent results.
